# Last updated: 2026-07-03
# Muhammad Ubaid Raza - AI Agent Architect & Full-Stack Consultant

> Senior Software Engineer (13+ years) specializing in AI agent systems, LLM orchestration, Laravel, Nuxt, and scalable SaaS architecture. Helping startups and teams build MVPs, AI-powered products, and production-ready systems through async-first consulting. 125+ projects delivered with 100% client satisfaction.

Key information about this website:
- This is a personal portfolio, blog, and consulting landing page for Muhammad Ubaid Raza
- The site is fully static (pre-rendered via Nuxt 4 + Nitro), built with Vue 3, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, and Nuxt UI 3
- Content is authored in Markdown/YAML and typed via Zod schemas through @nuxt/content
- The site owner is available for freelance, consulting, and collaborative projects - currently accepting new clients
- Response time: ~3 hours; Timezone: GMT+5
- Pricing: MVP development $5K-$15K USD, Architecture audit $1.5K-$3K USD, Hourly $100-$125 USD
- Payment terms: 20% upfront, rest on milestones

## About

- [Home - Muhammad Ubaid Raza](https://mubaidr.js.org/): Portfolio landing page with hero, stats, featured projects, testimonials, and call-to-action

## Services

- [Full-Stack Development](https://mubaidr.js.org/consulting): Vue.js/Nuxt, React/Next.js, Node.js, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, MongoDB - $100+/hr, 2-12 weeks
- [Technical Consulting](https://mubaidr.js.org/consulting): Tech stack selection, architecture design, code review, performance analysis, scalability planning - $120+/hr, 1-4 weeks
- [Project Leadership](https://mubaidr.js.org/consulting): Team leadership, agile delivery, mentoring, code review for startups and enterprises
- [Browser Extension Development](https://mubaidr.js.org/consulting): Chrome/Firefox extensions, Vite + Vue 3 tooling, cross-browser compatibility
- [API Development & Integration](https://mubaidr.js.org/consulting): RESTful/GraphQL APIs, Stripe, payment gateways, third-party integrations

## Projects

- [ParagonSquare.ai](https://mubaidr.js.org/projects): Multi-marketplace platform combining auctions, products, services, and rentals with 0% commission subscription model. 28K+ signups, 600K+ visitors across 5 countries
- [AI Document Chat Platform](https://mubaidr.js.org/projects): AI-powered document search with OCR, vector search, and RAG. 50K+ documents processed, 200K+ queries, 95% accuracy
- [WhatsApp Chat Summary](https://mubaidr.js.org/projects): AI platform connecting WhatsApp accounts via QR code for chat summarization, RAG-based Q&A, and reply suggestions
- [Vacation Rental Marketplace](https://mubaidr.js.org/projects): WebSocket-powered platform with 3D Secure payments and fraud detection. 10K+ properties, 50K+ guests, 99.9% uptime
- [Gem Team](https://github.com/mubaidr/gem-team): Multi-agent AI development framework with DAG-based orchestration, specialized agents, and production-grade workflows

## Blog - Recent & Featured Posts

- [Production-Grade AI Agents Are Not Prompts - They Are Systems](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/37-production-grade-ai-agents-not-prompts): Why real production agents require orchestration layers, not prompt chains
- [DAG-Based AI Orchestration: Why Linear Agent Pipelines Break at Scale](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/38-dag-ai-orchestration-pipelines-break): DAG-based orchestration beats sequential chains for production agent systems
- [I Stopped Building Features First - I Now Design Execution Systems First](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/39-execution-systems-first-not-features): Why designing execution systems first produces better software with less rework
- [Async-First Engineering in 2026: Why I Avoid Most Technical Calls](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/40-async-first-engineering-2026): Why senior engineering work is increasingly async-first
- [Multi-Agent Memory Systems: What Actually Works in Production](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/41-multi-agent-memory-systems-production): What actually works for multi-agent memory - structured state, controlled injection, lifecycle management
- [Laravel + AI Agent Systems: Building Hybrid Backends in 2026](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/42-laravel-ai-agent-hybrid-backends): Laravel as orchestration backend for AI agents with queue-driven execution
- [Nuxt 3 to Nuxt 4 Migration Guide: Complete Checklist for 2026](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/22-nuxt-3-to-4-migration-guide-2026): Step-by-step migration guide with codemod tools and troubleshooting
- [AI SEO for Developer Blogs: Get Cited by ChatGPT, Gemini & Perplexity in 2026](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/23-ai-seo-developer-blog-guide-2026): Complete guide to AI Search Engine Optimization for developer blogs
- [End-to-End Type Safety: The Complete Guide for 2026](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/27-end-to-end-type-safety-guide-2026): End-to-end type safety with TypeScript, Zod, and modern tools
- [Nuxt 4 SSR Performance Optimization: Complete Guide to Core Web Vitals](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/16-nuxt-4-ssr-performance-optimization): Nuxt 4 SSR performance strategies for Core Web Vitals
- [React Compiler and the End of Manual Memoization](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/21-react-compiler-manual-memoization-end): How React Compiler automates memoization in 2026
- [TypeScript 2026: New Features Every Developer Should Know](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/18-typescript-2026-new-features): Groundbreaking TypeScript features released in 2026
- [Design Tokens & Tailwind v4: The Complete Guide](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/29-design-tokens-tailwind-v4-guide): Design tokens and Tailwind CSS v4 for scalable design systems
- [WebAssembly in 2026: The Performance Revolution](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/25-webassembly-performance-revolution-2026): WebAssembly enabling near-native speed for web applications
- [Full-Stack Development in 2026: Complete Roadmap](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/33-full-stack-development-2026-roadmap): Modern full-stack development roadmap covering frameworks, tools, and best practices
- [Meta-Frameworks in 2026: The New Default for Web Development](https://mubaidr.js.org/blog/26-meta-frameworks-new-default-2026): Why meta-frameworks like Next.js, Nuxt, and SvelteKit dominate
